message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): tighten tick-313/314 pre-cancel assertions (tick 396)

Pre-tick-387: ticks 313 + 314 (pre-cancelled cx on GET + POST)
asserted only `result.is_err()` + `elapsed < 5s`. That was the
right loose contract when the HTTP client's cancel-observation
behavior was unclear.

Post-tick-387 ft-l9mxa fix: `DistributedHttpClient::{get,post}`
now have a pre-flight `cx.is_cancel_requested()` short-circuit
that returns `Err(ClientError::Cancelled)` deterministically —
no TCP connect, no server interaction, no possible other error
variants.

Tightened both tests to the specific contract:
- Pattern match `Err(asupersync::http::h1::http_client::ClientError::Cancelled)`
  exactly (not any Err).
- `elapsed < 1s` (was < 5s) — the pre-flight short-circuit is
  nearly instantaneous since no IO happens.

Why now: the loose contract was fine initially but less useful
for regression detection. A regression that removed the pre-flight
check (or broke the race_with_cx_cancel wiring such that pre-cancel
went through the normal path) would now be caught with a focused
diagnostic, not a generic "some error happened".

This also distinguishes pre-cancel from mid-flight cancel:
- Pre-cancel (ticks 313, 314): pre-flight check, no IO, <1s Cancelled.
- Mid-flight cancel (ticks 380, 389): cancel-watcher race, some IO
  started, ~70ms Cancelled.

Both converge on Cancelled but via different paths — tight
assertions make the paths separately verifiable.

Test result: 2/2 pass (both under 100ms per the tightened bound).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
